name: CalFresh (SNAP)
administered_by: CA Dept. of Social Services / SF Human Services Agency
short_description: Monthly grocery benefit on an EBT card for low-income households.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative average monthly benefit, modeled as 55% of the CDSS maximum monthly allotment (most households do not receive the max, which is reserved for $0 net-income households). Flagged as an estimate in the UI.
value_estimate._source: CDSS 2025-2026 CalFresh Income & Eligibility Limits, effective 10/1/2025-9/30/2026. Item #220000.

required_documents: Proof of identity (ID card, driver's license, or passport)
Proof of income (recent pay stubs, or a signed statement if paid in cash)
Proof of California/SF residency (lease, utility bill, or piece of mail)

name: PG&E CARE (California Alternate Rates for Energy)
short_description: 20%+ discount on gas and 35%+ discount on electric bills for qualifying households.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative flat annual savings estimate based on a representative SF household bill and PG&E's published discount (20%+ gas / 35%+ electric). Actual savings vary by usage and vintage of home.
value_estimate._source: PG&E CARE program page, income guidelines valid through 5/31/2027. https://www.pge.com/en/account/billing-and-assistance/financial-assistance/california-alternate-rates-for-energy-program.html

required_documents: Proof of income for every adult household member with income — most recent pay stub(s), an award letter (Social Security/SSDI/VA/pension), or a full federal tax return
OR proof of enrollment in a qualifying public assistance program (Medi-Cal, CalFresh, SSI, CalWORKs, LIHEAP) as a recent award or participation letter
_documents_source: PG&E Required Income Document Guide / Required Public Assistance Document Guide.

name: Free Muni for Seniors & People with Disabilities
short_description: Free Clipper-based Muni access for SF resident seniors (65+) and people with qualifying disabilities.

value_estimate._note: Value of a standard Muni-only ("M") monthly Clipper pass, waived entirely for enrolled clients, times 12.

required_documents: Proof of age 65+ (for seniors) via a Senior Clipper card, OR an active Clipper Access (RTC) card confirming a qualifying disability

application.form_name: Free Muni for Seniors & People with Disabilities Enrollment

name: Medi-Cal (Adult Expansion)
administered_by: CA Dept. of Health Care Services / SF Human Services Agency
short_description: Free or low-cost health coverage for low-income California adults 19-64. Note: as of 1/1/2026, new undocumented adult applicants get restricted-scope coverage only, not full-scope — this screener flags uncertain immigration status for review rather than guessing which scope applies.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative estimate using DHCS's average total Medi-Cal spending per enrollee (all funding sources), not a cash payment — actual value is health coverage, not dollars in hand.
value_estimate._source: KFF, Medicaid in California fact sheet (May 2025), FY2024 average cost of $10,100/enrollee. https://files.kff.org/attachment/fact-sheet-medicaid-state-CA

required_documents: Proof of income for all household members
Immigration status documents (determines full-scope vs. restricted-scope coverage)

name: SSI/SSP (Supplemental Security Income / State Supplementary Payment)
administered_by: Social Security Administration / CA Dept. of Social Services (state supplement)
short_description: Monthly cash benefit for people 65+, blind, or disabled with limited income and resources; California adds a state supplement on top of the federal payment.

value_estimate._note: Combined federal SSI + CA SSP maximum monthly payment for one person living independently, times 12. Actual determination uses SSA's own countable-income/resource test with exclusions this screener does not model (asset limit ~$2,000 individual) — couples receive more (~$25,186/yr combined).
value_estimate._source: SSA 2026 COLA fact sheet (effective Dec 2025 payments) + CA SSP payment standard, $1,233.94/mo individual independent-living rate. https://www.ssa.gov/news/en/press/releases/2025-10-24.html

required_documents: Medical evidence of disability, if applicable

name: CAPI (Cash Assistance Program for Immigrants)
administered_by: CA Dept. of Social Services / SF Human Services Agency
short_description: State-funded cash aid mirroring SSI/SSP for aged, blind, or disabled noncitizens who are ineligible for SSI solely because of their immigration status.

value_estimate._note: CAPI mirrors the SSI/SSP payment standard for one person living independently. Actual determination requires proof of an SSI denial due to immigration status within the prior 6 months, plus SSA-style income/asset exclusions this screener does not model.
value_estimate._source: CDSS CAPI program page, 2026 payment standards. https://www.cdss.ca.gov/capi

required_documents: SSI denial letter (due to immigration status)
Rental agreement or proof of living arrangement

name: In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS)
administered_by: CA Dept. of Social Services / SF Human Services Agency
short_description: Funds an in-home caregiver (help with bathing, cooking, mobility, etc.) for aged, blind, or disabled Californians who are Medi-Cal eligible and live in their own home.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative: statewide average authorized hours/month times SF's IHSS provider wage, times 12. Actual hours are set by an individualized in-home functional needs assessment, not an income formula.
value_estimate._source: LAO, The 2026-27 Budget: In-Home Supportive Services (127 hrs/mo statewide average, 2026-27) x SFHSA IHSS provider wage $23.00/hr (2026). https://lao.ca.gov/Publications/Report/5166 ; https://www.sfhsa.org/services/disability-aging/home-care/home-supportive-services-ihss/provide-home-supportive-services-ihss/become-ihss-provider/ihss-provider-wages

name: County Adult Assistance Programs (CAAP / General Assistance)
short_description: SF's cash aid of last resort for low-income adults without minor children in the household. Note: this screener cannot verify the "no minor children" requirement or the program's asset test — apply directly for a real determination.

value_estimate._note: Current maximum monthly CAAP cash grant, times 12. Actual net-income ceiling (~$714/mo) and a personal-property/asset limit ($2,000 single) are not modeled here.

name: PG&E FERA (Family Electric Rate Assistance)
short_description: 18% discount on the electric portion of your bill for households above CARE's income limit but below FERA's — a step up from PG&E CARE, not stackable with it.

value_estimate._note: Average annual savings across PG&E's FERA-enrolled customers, most recently published figure.
value_estimate._source: PG&E 2024 program data: 39,000+ enrolled customers saved $21M+ total, averaging ~$45/month. https://www.stocktitan.net/news/PCG/expanding-access-more-pg-e-customers-eligible-for-18-electric-l9gqm2gpw25n.html

required_documents: Proof of household income (or self-attestation under penalty of perjury)

name: LIHEAP Home Energy Assistance (via SFPES)
administered_by: CA Dept. of Community Services & Development, delivered locally by San Francisco-Peninsula Energy Services (SFPES)
short_description: Once-per-year utility bill credit, plus crisis/disconnection-prevention help, for low-income households — funding-limited, so income eligibility does not guarantee assistance.

value_estimate._note: Midpoint of CSD's published heating-benefit range ($94-$1,500); this screener does not model the 60%-state-median-income test or the priority-plan funding limits.

required_documents: Government photo ID for head of household
Proof of income for past 30 days, or proof of categorical program participation

name: California LifeLine
short_description: Discounted phone (landline/mobile) service for low-income households, plus a limited Home Broadband Pilot offering up to $30/month off internet.

value_estimate._note: Conservative baseline of the standard phone discount only; the Home Broadband Pilot (up to $30/mo) is a limited-status add-on not included here.
value_estimate._source: CPUC California LifeLine eligibility page, 2026-2027 table. https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/consumer-support/financial-assistance-savings-and-discounts/lifeline/california-lifeline-eligibility

required_documents: Proof of income, OR proof of enrollment in a qualifying categorical program

name: SFPUC Water/Sewer Customer Assistance Program
short_description: 25-40% discount on combined water & sewer bills for lower-income households. Important: only individually-metered single-family accounts qualify — most master-metered apartment buildings in SF are not eligible even if the resident's income qualifies.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative: 25% (the wider ≤50% AMI tier) off a representative SF combined water/sewer bill. Households at ≤30% AMI get a deeper 40% discount not separately modeled here.

required_documents: SFPUC account in applicant's name (individually-metered — not master-metered to the building)
Income documentation, or CalWORKs/CalFresh/Medi-Cal enrollment for a data-match waiver

name: SF Emergency Rental Assistance Program (SF ERAP)
administered_by: MOHCD / Dept. of Homelessness and Supportive Housing, funded via Season of Sharing
short_description: One-time back-rent, move-in cost, or limited future-rent help for SF renters at risk of eviction or housing loss due to financial hardship. Funding-limited — income eligibility does not guarantee assistance.

value_estimate._note: Maximum back-rent assistance (the program's primary use case); move-in assistance (up to $6,000) and future rent are additional, not included here.
value_estimate._source: sf.gov SF ERAP program rules, accessed 2026. https://www.sf.gov/information--sf-erap-program-rules

required_documents: Income verification for all adult household members
Proof of unpaid rent or move-in cost

name: DAHLIA Below Market Rate (BMR) Housing Lottery
administered_by: Mayor's Office of Housing and Community Development (MOHCD)
short_description: Weekly-rotating lottery for below-market-rate rental and ownership units across SF. Always something new to apply to, but each listing is a low-odds lottery, not a guaranteed unit.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative gap between SF's 2026 average market 1BR rent (~$3,600-4,000/mo per Zillow/Zumper) and a representative 80%-AMI BMR-restricted rent computed from the SF AMI table. Highly variable by unit and listing.
value_estimate._source: DAHLIA San Francisco Housing Portal. https://housing.sfgov.org/

required_documents: Documentation requested only if selected in the lottery

name: Clipper START
administered_by: Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC), region-wide via Clipper
short_description: 50% off single-ride transit fares across nearly all Bay Area operators (Muni, BART, Caltrain, SamTrans, and more) for eligible low-income adults 19-64.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative annual savings for a regular transit rider getting 50% off single-ride fares; actual value scales with ridership.
value_estimate._source: SFMTA Clipper START page; MTC vote making the pilot an ongoing fare product (5/28/2025). https://www.sfmta.com/fares/clipper-start

required_documents: EBT card, Medi-Cal card, or county benefits-eligibility letter, OR most recent federal tax return

application._apply_mode_note: Was "web_submit" until the 2026-07-11 live walk of clipperstartcard.com/s/application: saving the first section immediately redirects to a passwordless email/SMS OTP screen, and Profile Information, Mailing Address, Verification Method, and the required Proof of Identity/Income uploads are all unreachable until that code is entered. A headless worker cannot read a one-time code out of the applicant's real inbox, so there is no automation path — not a stub. See worker/README.md.

name: Clipper Access (RTC Discount ID Card)
administered_by: Metropolitan Transportation Commission (511.org), fulfilled via Clipper
short_description: Discount fare ID primarily for people with disabilities, giving reduced regional transit fares across Bay Area operators — no income test. Note: this screener's senior-or-disabled check is shared with Free Muni and will also pass on age 65+ alone, though the real program is disability-focused; a senior without a qualifying disability should confirm directly. Rebranded from "RTC Card" in December 2025.

value_estimate._note: Illustrative annual value of discounted regional transit fares for a regular rider; varies by operator and usage.
value_estimate._source: 511.org Clipper Access (formerly RTC Card) program page, rebranded 12/10/2025. https://511.org/travel/transit/clipper-access

required_documents: DMV disabled parking placard registration, ADA Paratransit eligibility, qualifying VA disability rating, or a Medical Certification form completed by a CA-licensed professional

name: CalEITC (California Earned Income Tax Credit)
short_description: Refundable state tax credit for low-income working individuals and families, claimed on your CA state tax return (not a separate application). Worth up to $302 with no qualifying children, or up to $3,756 with qualifying children — this screener shows a conservative illustrative estimate.

value_estimate._note: Conservative illustrative figure between the $302 (no qualifying children) and $3,756 (with qualifying children) tax-year-2025 maximums. This screener compares total gross income to the cap as a proxy — CalEITC's real test is on earned income specifically.
value_estimate._source: FTB CalEITC eligibility page, tax year 2025 (filed 2026). https://www.ftb.ca.gov/file/personal/credits/caleitc/eligibility-and-credit-information.html

required_documents: SSN or ITIN for filer, spouse, and qualifying children

application.form_name: FTB Form 3514, filed with CA Form 540
